articles.headline: Weekly Intelligence Report highlights ransomware, vulnerabilities, and data leaks
articles.summary: CYFIRMA’s weekly report describes ransomware and leak activity affecting a transportation and logistics company in Singapore and a construction company in Japan. Those sectors are operationally important for many businesses, so the report is a reminder that supply-chain disruption and third-party cyber risk remain immediate business concerns.[5]

articles.headline: US tech stocks’ market dominance reaches new heights and raises fresh risks
articles.summary: Reuters says the AI-driven buildout has lifted profit estimates for semiconductor and other tech companies, but it also concentrated market risk in a small group of firms. For businesses, that matters because chip, cloud, and AI infrastructure spending can move sharply with investor expectations, affecting supplier pricing and availability.[7]

articles.headline: Is A.I. replacing tech workers or providing an excuse for job cuts?
articles.summary: The New York Times reports that tech layoffs are accelerating and executives are increasingly attributing cuts to AI-driven productivity gains. That is relevant beyond the tech sector because it signals how employers may justify restructuring, automation, and workforce reductions across industries.[9]
